title: XCA menu bar items highlight but submenus don’t appear when running under RealVNC (LXDE); works on HDMI
author: HeinzRuetschi
body:
Summary

When XCA is started inside a RealVNC session on the Raspberry Pi (Debian 13 + LXDE), the menu bar shows File/Extra/Help, but clicking them only highlights the top-level item; the submenu popup never appears. The same XCA version works correctly when run on the Pi with a physical HDMI monitor + keyboard.
Environment

    XCA: 2.8.0
    OS: Debian GNU/Linux 13 (Linux 6.18.34+rpt), Raspberry Pi 5
    Desktop: LXDE
    RealVNC Server: 7.13.1 (r57) on the Pi
    RealVNC Viewer: 7.15.1 (r18) on Windows 10

Installation

Installed via: Pi menu → Preferences → Add/Remove Software → xca
Reproduction steps (RealVNC session)

    Connect to the Pi using RealVNC Viewer (Windows 10).
    On LXDE desktop, open XCA via Pi menu → Accessories → XCA.
    In XCA, click the menu bar items: File (also occurs with Extra/Help).

Expected result

Submenus (File/Extra/Help) appear and submenu items are selectable/clickable.
Actual result

The clicked top-level menu item becomes highlighted/selected, but no submenu/popup items appear.
Control

Running XCA with the same Pi using a physical HDMI monitor + keyboard: submenus appear and XCA works normally.
Notes / Attempts

    Tried running XCA with different Qt theme env vars (e.g., unsetting QT_QPA_PLATFORMTHEME); menu issue persists in RealVNC session.

title: Migration error...
author: AchMol
body:
Hi all,
I try to migrate a xca instance with mysql database to postgres database. Both databases are in the network. First I tried to export the files with "./Export/Datenbank ausgeben", but this is not the right way because i have to enter the password at every certificate during import of all 3000 certificates.

After that I migrated with pgloader from mysql to postgres. That worked fine, the table are filled without errors. I can see the data with psql. But when I connect to the new postgres database with xca the connect is working but the windows are all empty without errors.

What happened here and what ist wrong? I've also seen that all views in the database are empty.

Any hint?

title: importing PFX file into MACOS Keychain_different error messsages
author: azur538
body: I have successfully installed a pfx file on Windows. I have saved the file on a USB stick and transferred it on my hard disk (MAC NEO). First, I had the following message after entering the password "impossible to decode the provided data". However, I have tried opening the file directly from the email sent on my MAC with the extension p12, the message is different "invalid password". I tried again and I got another message "system root keychain cannot be modified". I tried to modify trust settings as instructed but nothing happened. At some point I also had a unidentified key with no information. The key vanished when I tried again later (Above). Tried again one more time from the p12 file. For the first time, the dialog box asked me "do you want to add the certificate of this file to a keychain?" but when I typed the password, I got 2 successive messages 1/ "invalid password" 2/"the required administrator authorization was not provided"

title: Windows - Only installed for the current user
author: wasamolle
body:
I can install and use xca just fine in Windows Server 2019.
But when I log in as another user, there are no links in the start menu, and, what baffles me the most, no entry in programs and features.

I know that I can create or move the links manually, but I would prefer if xca would be installed globally (optional).

title: Authority Key Identifier problems
author: ciupol
body:
There seems to be a problem with Authority Key Identifier when creating self-signed certificates. When I check the box on extensions tab the Authority Key Identifier is not present on the generated certificate. If I uncheck the box and add `authorityKeyIdentifier = keyid, issuer:always` or `authorityKeyIdentifier = keyid:always` or `authorityKeyIdentifier = keyid:always, issuer:always` in the advanced tab the identifier is also not present. When I add `authorityKeyIdentifier = issuer:always` in advanced tab the certificate will have Authority Key Identifier details but no key id. When I enter `authorityKeyIdentifier = keyid` the Authority Key Identifier field is present in generated certificate but the keyid is 0.
All seems to be working correctly when generating not self-signed certificate.

title: Fail to build against OpenSSL 4.0
author: teward
body:
OpenSSL 4.0 is out, and there is no support available for it in XCA.

This was reported downstream in Debian.  The contents of the [Debian bug report is here](https://bugs.debian.org/cgi-bin/bugreport.cgi?bug=1138318):

```plain
OpenSSL 4.0 is in Debian experimental. This package fails to build against it:

| [  5%] Building CXX object CMakeFiles/xcalib.dir/lib/pki_pkcs12.cpp.o
| /usr/bin/c++ -DOPENSSL_SUPPRESS_DEPRECATED -DQT_CORE_LIB -DQT_GUI_LIB -DQT_HELP_LIB -DQT_MESSAGELOGCONTEXT -DQT_NO_DEBUG -DQT_SQL_LIB -DQT_WIDGETS_LIB -I/build/reproducible-path/xca-2.9.0/obj-x86_64-linux-gnu/xcalib_autogen/include -I/build/reproducible-path/xca-2.9.0/obj-x86_64-linux-gnu -I/build/reproducible-path/xca-2.9.0 -isystem /usr/include/x86_64-linux-gnu/qt6/QtWidgets -isystem /usr/include/x86_64-linux-gnu/qt6 -isystem /usr/include/x86_64-linux-gnu/qt6/QtCore -isystem /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/qt6/mkspecs/linux-g++ -isystem /usr/include/x86_64-linux-gnu/qt6/QtGui -isystem /usr/include/x86_64-linux-gnu/qt6/QtSql -isystem /usr/include/x86_64-linux-gnu/qt6/QtHelp -g -O2 -ffile-prefix-map=/build/reproducible-path/xca-2.9.0=. -fstack-protector-strong -fstack-clash-protection -Wformat -Werror=format-security -fcf-protection -Wdate-time -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=2 -std=gnu++17 -fPIC -Wall -Wextra -Wno-unknown-pragmas -fmacro-prefix-map=/build/reproducible-path/xca-2.9.0=. -MD -MT CMakeFiles/xcalib.dir/lib/pki_pkcs12.cpp.o -MF CMakeFiles/xcalib.dir/lib/pki_pkcs12.cpp.o.d -o CMakeFiles/xcalib.dir/lib/pki_pkcs12.cpp.o -c /build/reproducible-path/xca-2.9.0/lib/pki_pkcs12.cpp
| /build/reproducible-path/xca-2.9.0/lib/pki_pkcs12.cpp: In constructor â€˜pki_pkcs12::pki_pkcs12(const QString&)â€™:
| /build/reproducible-path/xca-2.9.0/lib/pki_pkcs12.cpp:88:53: error: invalid conversion from â€˜const unsigned char*â€™ to â€˜unsigned char*â€™ [-fpermissive]
|    88 |                 unsigned char *str = X509_alias_get0(mycert, NULL);
|       |                                      ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^~~~~~~~~~~~~~
|       |                                                     |
|       |                                                     const unsigned char*
| make[4]: *** [CMakeFiles/xcalib.dir/build.make:215: CMakeFiles/xcalib.dir/lib/pki_pkcs12.cpp.o] Error 1
```

Full build log from the original reporter's build environment is attached here.

[xca_2.9.0-1_amd64-2026-04-19T09_00_53Z.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28450501/xca_2.9.0-1_amd64-2026-04-19T09_00_53Z.log)

title: Microsoft Store (UWP) XCA version cannot load PKCS#11 .dll module that has dependencies
author: ARGYROU-MINAS
body:
Microsoft Store UWP apps are restricted and run in their own "sandbox". The Microsoft Store version of XCA has the option to use the File Picker to load an external PKCS#11 .dll module.

However, that only works when the .dll module is a single file. When the .dll module itself has dependencies, this won't work since the dependencies won't be included in the "sandbox".

Out of the many PKCS#11 .dll modules I'm using, only 1 has this issue; but it is the one from Yubico (ykcs11.dll), which has 2 dependencies.

Installing the standalone XCA version obviates this issue.

title: Please add support for `.pgpass` and psql client environment variables
author: Gunni
body: To connect to remote databases.

title: Syntax for `remote database descriptor` undocumented
author: Gunni
body: The man page says I can use `--database` and them some magic spell after that to connect to a remote database, but no examples are given.

title: Intermediate CAs orphaned when renewing root CA?
author: StolkB
body: I set up a Root CA with two intermediate CAs: one for SSL and one for VPN clients. When I try to renew the root certificate (with 'replace old certificate' checked), I get the popup with "Succesfully created the certificate ... etc" After I click OK on the popup, the old certificate is deleted. It seems however that in that proces the link between Root CA and intermediate CAs is broken, and both intermediate CAs are now top level. When I right-click on the intermediate CAs, there is no 'renewal' option in the dropdown menu. How can I renew the Root CA without breaking the link to the intermediate CAs? Maybe I configured something wrong when building the root and intermediate certificates? They have different private keys, different internal and common names, I set the basic constraints for all to CA, and key usage to Certificate Sign, CRL Sign. The intermediate CAs were signed with the root CA. Thanks, Bart

title: Feature Request: Export of ECC keys in SSH2 format
author: ARGYROU-MINAS
body:
XCA currently has the option to export RSA keys in SSH2 format (private, public).

The same functionality is missing for ECC keys; it should be added.

title: Support for extKU for Ipsec - id-kp-ipsecIKE
author: OlegAndrianov
body:
The tools does not support IPsec EKU that is required by  active regulation.

Currently the authoritative [RFC 4945](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc4945)   states:

```
Implementations that do support EKU MUST support the following logic for certificate validation:
   o  If no EKU extension, continue.
   o  If EKU present AND contains either id-kp-ipsecIKE or
      anyExtendedKeyUsage, continue.
   o  Otherwise, reject cert.
```

Current international certification requirements also allow use on this extension value (https://www.niap-ccevs.org/static_html/protection-profile/511/Functional%20Package%20for%20X.509_v1.0.html
```
extendedKeyUsage is present and is populated with [selection:
serverAuth {OID: 1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.1}
clientAuth {OID: 1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.2}
emailProtection {OID: 1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.4}
**ipsecIKE {OID: 1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.17}**
codeSigning {OID: 1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.3}
```

Can you please add that to the tool? I would be  happy to get a nightly build -)

XCA does support extensions 
id-kp-ipsecEndSystem 
id-kp-ipsecTunnel 
id-kp-ipsecUser
that are listed as obsolete by RFC 4945 (already obsolete in 2007).

Thanks /hans

title: Certificates List slow if "Key Type" or "Key Size" or "common name" columns are enabled
author: gsemia
body:
I have recently updated from xca 2.4 to 2.9 and the Certificates Lists felt very slow.

I have ~800 certificates from ~40 ca in my xca database

I found out that if the "Key type" or "Key size" or "common name" column is enabled in the certificates list, it makes it extremely slow and sluggish..

When I disable all three columns it feels considerably faster

I usually have the following columns enabled:

Internal name
Date
CommonName
Key name
Key type
Key size
CA
Start date
Expiry date
CRL Expiration

title: Invalidity Date added to the CRL
author: Sefyupowa
body:
Hello, 
while importing an existsing PKI in XCA, i imported also the CRLs to keep track of the revoked certificates.
and i noticed a change in the CRL, an extension `2.5.29.24 - Invalidity Date` is added on the CRLs generated with XCA.
the issue is, since the previously revoked certificates don't have this extension, the value is set to:

```
Extensions: 1
    2.5.29.24: Flags = 0, Length = 11
    Invalidity Date:  1/1/10000 12:59 AM
```
Is there any way, to make this extension optional or remove it from the CRLs?

title: [Feature] Utilize and Implement PQ-encryption methods
author: teward
body:
With various policies at NIST and government levels in place, by 2030 all PKI utilized by the government and civilian industry sectors under those standards will need to implement Post-Quantum Encryption and Signature Methods.

Currently, it is my understanding these signature algorithms are available with OpenSSL 3.5 (which is available in Debian Testing and Unstable and Experimental, and is in Ubuntu 25.10 and will be in 26.04).

Additionally, it is suggested by current post-quantum transitions ahead of a cryptographically-significant quantum computer being available to use composite signatures as an option, so legacy items can still use generated certificates while newer signature algorithms are also accepted, as part of this transition.

Does XCA support these signature algos when built against a version of OpenSSL 3.5 which contains the post-quantum encryption and signature algorithms, and if not can we get it implemented?

Additionally, can we implement hybrid/composite signatures so both post-quantum and current signature algos can be used at the same time?

title: improving xca command line options?
author: harridu
body:
Suggestion about using xca on the command line, eg
```
xca --list-items --database "${XCA_DB}" --password env:XCA_PASSPHRASE
```

Showing just an internal identifier, the item type and the item name might be insufficient to find the object you are looking for. I would like to see the CN and ignore the expired certificates, for example. Maybe something like 
```
xca --list-certificates --valid --database ...
xca --select  $id  --format "<formatstring>" --database ...
```
?

title: QCommandLineParser: option not expecting values: "pem"
author: harridu
body:
Exporting a CRL on the command line I get a warning
```
% xca /home/ca/example_ca.xdb --password=env:password --crlgen=ipsec-ca --pem
   000 Warning: QCommandLineParser: option not expecting values: "pem"
```
--pem is last in line, so obviously there is something weird. The CRL is generated, though.

title: Improving Database Performance
author: lunakoa
body:
I use mariadb as the backend database on Rocky Linux 9 for my XCADB 2.9, as my entries grow it gets more sluggish.

- Is there a way to improve database performance?  
- Can I turn on indexing to speed up queries?  If so what should I index on.
- Is using an odbc connector better than using native connector?

Thanks for you help.

LA

title: No debug output when using XCA_DEBUG
author: m8522s
body:
Hi team,

I try to generate debug output while troubleshooting a PKCS#11 module.
The command is `XCA_DEBUG=all xca`. However, there is no output in the journal or in the console, except for warnings and errors.

The used version is 2.9.0 on Fedora 43.

Please point me in the right direction.

Thanks.
